annotations.label: Homotopy classes / nth homotopy group
annotations.note: `HomotopyGroup.Pi n X x` (notation `π_ n X x`) is the quotient of generalized loops by boundary-relative homotopy.

annotations.label: Equivalent cube definition of π_n
annotations.note: `GenLoop N X x` is exactly the cube definition: continuous maps `I^N → X` sending the boundary to `x`.

annotations.label: Sum of maps (cube definition)

anchor.snippet: We therefore define the sum of maps

annotations.note: `GenLoop.transAt i f g` concatenates two cube-based loops along the `i`-th coordinate, giving the group multiplication on `HomotopyGroup`.

annotations.label: Sum of maps (sphere definition)

anchor.snippet: For the corresponding definition in terms of spheres

annotations.note: Mathlib's homotopy groups are built from cubes, not from `S^n` with an equator-pinching map, and no such sphere-based sum is defined.

annotations.label: Higher homotopy groups are abelian
annotations.note: The `CommGroup (HomotopyGroup N X x)` instance for `Nontrivial N` proves abelianness of `π_(n+2)` via the Eckmann–Hilton argument.

annotations.label: Basepoint independence for path-connected spaces

anchor.snippet: for a path-connected space any two choices of basepoint give rise to isomorphic

annotations.note: Only the fundamental-group case has a basepoint-change isomorphism via path conjugation; no analogue is proved for `HomotopyGroup` with `n ≥ 2`.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence of a fibration

anchor.section: Long exact sequence of a fibration
anchor.snippet: Then there is a long exact sequence of homotopy groups

annotations.note: No long exact sequence of homotopy groups associated to a (Serre or Hurewicz) fibration is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf fibration

anchor.section: Long exact sequence of a fibration

annotations.note: Grep for `Hopf` / `hopfFibration` in `Mathlib/` finds nothing; the Hopf map `S^3 → S^2` is not constructed.

annotations.label: Covering space homotopy groups

anchor.section: Long exact sequence of a fibration
anchor.snippet: In the case of a cover space, when the fiber is discrete

annotations.note: The homotopy-lifting property of covering maps is formalized, but the consequence that `π_n` of a cover equals `π_n` of the base for `n ≥ 2` is not stated.

annotations.label: Puppe sequence from mapping fibre/cone

anchor.section: Long exact sequence of a fibration
anchor.snippet: When the fibration is the mapping fibre

annotations.note: No mapping-fibre, mapping-cone, or Puppe sequence of homotopy groups is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SO fibration and low-order homotopy groups
annotations.note: No fibration `SO(n-1) → SO(n) → S^{n-1}` or computations of low-order homotopy groups of `SO(n)` are present.

annotations.label: Milnor's exotic spheres

anchor.snippet: he was able to find exotic spheres

annotations.note: Searching `exotic.*sphere` finds only a mention in `PoincareConjecture.lean`; Milnor's construction of exotic spheres is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fibration for complex projective space
annotations.note: No fibration `S^1 → S^{2n+1} → ℂP^n` and no resulting homotopy-group computations are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Aspherical spaces have trivial higher homotopy

anchor.snippet: For some spaces, such as tori

annotations.note: There is no notion of aspherical space or Eilenberg–MacLane space `K(G,1)` in Mathlib, and no triviality of `π_n` for `n ≥ 2` for tori or other aspherical spaces.

annotations.label: Hurewicz comparison for n-connected spaces
annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain the Hurewicz theorem comparing `π_n` and `H_n` (the only `Hurewicz` hit is for covering maps being Hurewicz fibrations).

annotations.label: Relative homotopy groups
annotations.note: No `RelativeHomotopyGroup` or relative `π_n(X, A, x_0)` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopic relative to A

anchor.snippet: The elements of such a group are homotopy classes of based maps

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.HomotopicRel f₀ f₁ S` formalizes homotopy of continuous maps relative to a subset, but it is not specialized to the relative-homotopy-group setting of triples `(X, A, x_0)`.

annotations.label: Relative groups: abelian / crossed module
annotations.note: Relative homotopy groups are not defined, and no `CrossedModule` structure (search returns nothing) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence of relative homotopy groups

anchor.snippet: There is also a long exact sequence of relative homotopy groups

annotations.note: Without relative homotopy groups defined, the associated long exact sequence of a pair is also absent from Mathlib.
